name: storage-debug-instrumentation
description: Add comprehensive debugging and observability tooling for backend storage layers (PostgreSQL, ChromaDB) and startup metrics. Includes storage drift detection, raw data inspection endpoints, and a Next.js admin dashboard.
capabilities: Inspect raw storage data from PostgreSQL and ChromaDB databases
Detect storage drift between databases with missing and dangling entry reports
Track startup phase timing including DB init, cache preload, and RSS refresh
Browse paginated articles with source filters and date range options
View storage summary metrics at a glance on a unified debug dashboard
author: BenderFendor
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) providing implementation instructions for debugging tools. No executable code, scripts, or network operations are present. All 103 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: the skill describes adding debug endpoints but contains no actual implementation code. URLs flagged are localhost development endpoints, and backticks are markdown code block syntax, not shell execution.
valueStatement: Storage issues in PostgreSQL and ChromaDB are hard to diagnose without visibility. This skill provides a complete debugging toolkit with endpoints and a dashboard for inspecting storage state, detecting drift, and tracking startup performance.
userTitle: Add debugging tools for storage layers
limitations: Requires implementing backend debug endpoints described in the skill
Requires Next.js frontend setup for the visual dashboard
Provides observability only - no automatic issue resolution
Dashboard requires web browser access to view

useCases.title: Debug storage synchronization
useCases.description: Identify articles missing from ChromaDB or dangling entries causing search issues

useCases.title: Monitor startup performance
useCases.description: Track database initialization, cache preload, and RSS refresh durations over time

useCases.title: Inspect storage state
useCases.description: Browse raw article data and verify storage layer contents during development

promptTemplates.title: Enable debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the storage-debug-instrumentation skill to add debugging endpoints and a dashboard for inspecting PostgreSQL and ChromaDB storage.

promptTemplates.title: Investigate storage issues
promptTemplates.prompt: My application is missing articles in search results. Use storage-debug-instrumentation to help me find articles that exist in PostgreSQL but are missing from ChromaDB.

promptTemplates.title: Review startup metrics
promptTemplates.prompt: My application started slowly. Use storage-debug-instrumentation to show me the startup timeline and identify which phase is taking the longest.

promptTemplates.title: Fix storage drift
promptTemplates.prompt: I found storage drift between my databases. Use storage-debug-instrumentation to show me samples of missing-in-chroma and dangling entries so I can fix the synchronization issue.

bestPractices: Secure debug endpoints with authentication before exposing in production
Use pagination when browsing large datasets to avoid performance impact
Monitor storage drift regularly to catch synchronization issues early
antiPatterns: Leaving debug endpoints publicly accessible without authentication
Manually checking for drift instead of using automated detection
Ignoring startup delays without investigating the root cause

faq.question: Which AI platforms work with this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for implementing debugging tools.

faq.question: How do I activate this skill?
faq.answer: Call the skill when you need to add or use debug endpoints for storage inspection.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: The skill describes accessing PostgreSQL and ChromaDB databases through debug endpoints.

faq.question: Does this skill send data externally?
faq.answer: No external network calls. Debug endpoints are local to your application.

faq.question: What are the usage limits?
faq.answer: No limits. Dashboard pagination handles large datasets efficiently.

faq.question: How is this different from other debugging tools?
faq.answer: Provides end-to-end storage visibility with drift detection across two database systems.
